item.title: PostHog reverse proxy via Cloudflare
item.intent: PostHog traffic must route through the site's own domain so ad blockers and privacy extensions don't block analytics — typically recovers 10-30% of lost events. An AI infrastructure site that can't measure its own audience is ironic.
item.description: Configure a Cloudflare Worker or Pages Function to proxy PostHog API requests through a first-party subdomain (e.g., e.simonplant.com or /ingest/ path rewrite). Update the api_host in posthog.init() to use the proxy endpoint instead of us.i.posthog.com. Ensure the proxy forwards all required PostHog endpoints (/e/, /decide/, /engage/, static assets).
steps: Create a Cloudflare Pages Function or Worker at /ingest/* that proxies requests to us.i.posthog.com, rewriting the path and forwarding headers
Proxy must handle: POST /ingest/e/ (events), POST /ingest/decide/ (feature flags), GET /ingest/array/* (JS bundle), POST /ingest/engage/ (surveys)
Update api_host in CookieConsent.astro posthog.init() to use the proxy path (e.g., api_host: '/ingest')
Test that events still arrive in PostHog dashboard via the proxied path
Document the proxy setup in a code comment for future reference

acceptanceCriteria.text: PostHog api_host points to first-party proxy, not us.i.posthog.com
Proxy route or worker file exists
acceptanceCriteria.verify: ls functions/ingest/ 2>/dev/null | grep -q . || test -f functions/ingest.ts || test -f workers/posthog-proxy.ts
